action_prescription.common_failure: The common failure is not lack of knowledge. It is remembering the tool only after the emotion is already high.
action_prescription.do_today: Today, use one low-risk version of this sentence: “I can see this is heavy for you. I can help you sort the next step, but I cannot carry the whole weight for you.”
action_prescription.repair_move: If you miss it, do not restart the whole plan. Review one trigger and one first sentence for next time.
action_prescription.script: I can see this is heavy for you. I can help you sort the next step, but I cannot carry the whole weight for you.

seven_day_plan.practice: List one recent moment when you carried someone else’s load.
Commit to only one specific support action.
Notice your body after stating a boundary.
Review whether the boundary actually harmed the relationship.
Choose one situation next week where you will not automatically take over.

action_prescription.title: Empathy with a Boundary
action_prescription.watch_out: Do not turn the practice into a demand to be perfectly steady. Aim for one extra pause or one clearer sentence.
action_prescription.why_this_matters: After understanding someone, clarify what you can support and what you cannot carry for them.

career_environment.do_not_overread: This describes an environment variable, not a role recommendation.
career_environment.fit_signal: Can work well for people who turn emotional information into communication and decisions, if boundaries are explicit.
career_environment.interview_question: Over time, does the environment allow pause, handoff, and emotional reset?
career_environment.label: Emotional labor: high
career_environment.meaning: how often the role asks you to receive, soothe, or organize other people’s emotion
career_environment.recovery_condition: Requires predictable space for pause, review, and handoff.
role_observation_checklist: Notice whether meetings have a clear purpose and ending point.
Notice whether feedback targets behavior rather than personal character.
Notice whether people can step out of emotionally loaded conversations when needed.
career_environment.strain_signal: The strain is sustained emotional load, which can drain judgment and recovery.
career_environment.team_risk: If this variable is ignored, teams often shift emotional cost onto the person most willing to carry it.
career_environment.what_to_verify: Over time, does the environment allow pause, handoff, and emotional reset?

career_environment.do_not_overread: This describes an environment variable, not a role recommendation.
career_environment.fit_signal: Useful when you can move between interaction and solo work with enough review time.
career_environment.interview_question: Does the team have clear meeting rhythm, feedback norms, and recovery windows?
career_environment.label: Autonomy and recovery space: medium
career_environment.meaning: whether you can create space for solitude, reflection, and emotional reset
career_environment.recovery_condition: Requires predictable space for pause, review, and handoff.
role_observation_checklist: Notice whether meetings have a clear purpose and ending point.
Notice whether feedback targets behavior rather than personal character.
Notice whether people can step out of emotionally loaded conversations when needed.
career_environment.strain_signal: The strain is that unclear boundaries can slowly eat into recovery time.
career_environment.team_risk: If this variable is ignored, teams often shift emotional cost onto the person most willing to carry it.
career_environment.what_to_verify: Does the team have clear meeting rhythm, feedback norms, and recovery windows?

core_formulation.core_claim: Your empathy is not the problem. The leverage point is what happens after empathy: companionship, responsibility, and recovery need clearer separation.
core_formulation.development_lever: Practice boundary language and recovery steps after empathic contact.
core_formulation.do_not_overread: This is an interpretive pattern, not a fixed identity label or capability credential.
evidence_basis: EM high; ER low or medium-low.
core_formulation.likely_cost: You may slide from “I understand this” into “I have to carry this with you or for you.”
core_formulation.one_liner: You often pick up other people’s feelings quickly, but you may not recover from carrying them just as quickly.
core_formulation.primary_strength: You can make people feel understood, especially when their emotion is obvious but not clearly stated.
core_formulation.share_line: You often pick up other people’s feelings quickly, but you may not recover from carrying them just as quickly.
core_formulation.title: High Empathy, Low Recovery

boundary.claim_boundary: Not for career prediction, hiring, clinical diagnosis, fixed identity labels, or emotional ability claims.
boundary.how_to_use: Treat MBTI, Big Five, and Enneagram as evidence from different questions. They do not rewrite EQ scores or replace the emotional and relational self-report result.
boundary.summary: This module only explains which related assessments are available and how they can support reading the EQ result.
boundary.title: Cross-assessment context boundary

mechanisms.cost: The risk is that insight becomes pressure, or that understanding stays in the head.
mechanisms.development_lever: Pick one recent conversation and identify where the chain broke: awareness, regulation, empathy, or relational action.
mechanisms.do_not_overread: This is an interaction pattern, not a fixed personality label.
mechanisms.micro_action: Next time, add just one step: name it first, then respond.
mechanisms.strength: It helps you find one small trainable link instead of vaguely trying to “get better.”
mechanisms.title: Empathy × Emotion Regulation: noticing more than can be carried
mechanisms.what_it_feels_like: When reading another person is strong but recovering yourself is weaker, the user may notice a lot but get stuck at the next step.
mechanisms.why_it_matters: This mechanism explains how two emotional systems connect or disconnect in real situations, not what one score means in isolation.

mechanisms.cost: The risk is that insight becomes pressure, or that understanding stays in the head.

mechanisms.strength: It helps you find one small trainable link instead of vaguely trying to “get better.”
mechanisms.title: Empathy × Relationship Management: noticing more than can be carried
mechanisms.what_it_feels_like: When understanding the feeling is strong but moving the relationship forward is weaker, the user may notice a lot but get stuck at the next step.
mechanisms.why_it_matters: This mechanism explains how two emotional systems connect or disconnect in real situations, not what one score means in isolation.

personalization_route.next_best_action: Read the selected scenes first, then practice one prescription for seven days.
personalization_route.save_reason: Save this route so you can compare it with real interactions later.
personalization_route.why_this_feels_specific: This route organizes your strongest signals as High Empathy, Low Recovery and prioritizes the most relevant mechanisms, scenes, and practices.
personalization_route.why_this_route_exists: This route organizes your strongest signals as High Empathy, Low Recovery and prioritizes the most relevant mechanisms, scenes, and practices.

reality_scenes.better_move: Separate the feedback into three layers: facts, emotion, and next step. First confirm one concrete change.
reality_scenes.cost: You may slide from “I understand this” into “I have to carry this with you or for you.”
reality_scenes.do_not_overread: Use this as a scene lens, not a prediction of behavior.
reality_scenes.micro_script: “Let me check one thing first: what exactly would you like me to adjust?”
reality_scenes.reflection_prompt: In this situation, what were you trying to protect: the facts, the relationship, or your own recovery space?
reality_scenes.reflection_question: In this situation, what were you trying to protect: the facts, the relationship, or your own recovery space?
reality_scenes.result_page_depth: Use in Reality Translation cards and Agent follow-up prompts.
reality_scenes.strength: You can make people feel understood, especially when their emotion is obvious but not clearly stated.
reality_scenes.tiny_experiment: Separate the feedback into three layers: facts, emotion, and next step. First confirm one concrete change.
reality_scenes.title: High Empathy, Low Recovery in feedback
reality_scenes.typical_response: When someone points out an issue, asks for changes, or evaluates your work, your first response may be shaped by this pattern: You often pick up other people’s feelings quickly, but you may not recover from carrying them just as quickly. You may register tone, expectation, or disappointment before the specific information.
reality_scenes.why_this_matters: This scene translates your EQ pattern into a concrete everyday moment.

reality_scenes.better_move: Name the point of disagreement before the fight becomes about winning. Shift from “How could you?” to “This is where we are stuck.”
reality_scenes.cost: You may slide from “I understand this” into “I have to carry this with you or for you.”
reality_scenes.do_not_overread: Use this as a scene lens, not a prediction of behavior.
reality_scenes.micro_script: “Let’s not make this bigger yet. What is the exact point we are stuck on?”
reality_scenes.reflection_prompt: In this situation, what were you trying to protect: the facts, the relationship, or your own recovery space?
reality_scenes.reflection_question: In this situation, what were you trying to protect: the facts, the relationship, or your own recovery space?
reality_scenes.result_page_depth: Use in Reality Translation cards and Agent follow-up prompts.
reality_scenes.strength: You can make people feel understood, especially when their emotion is obvious but not clearly stated.
reality_scenes.tiny_experiment: Name the point of disagreement before the fight becomes about winning. Shift from “How could you?” to “This is where we are stuck.”
reality_scenes.title: High Empathy, Low Recovery in conflict
reality_scenes.typical_response: When a disagreement gets tense and both sides want to prove their point, you may use your default pattern to protect either the relationship or yourself. Its strength is: You can make people feel understood, especially when their emotion is obvious but not clearly stated. The cost may be: You may slide from “I understand this” into “I have to carry this with you or for you.”
reality_scenes.why_this_matters: This scene translates your EQ pattern into a concrete everyday moment.

reality_scenes.better_move: Acknowledge the feeling first, then name the kind of support you can realistically offer.
reality_scenes.cost: You may slide from “I understand this” into “I have to carry this with you or for you.”
reality_scenes.do_not_overread: Use this as a scene lens, not a prediction of behavior.
reality_scenes.micro_script: “I can see this is heavy. What I can offer right now is..., but I cannot carry the whole thing for you.”
reality_scenes.reflection_prompt: In this situation, what were you trying to protect: the facts, the relationship, or your own recovery space?
reality_scenes.reflection_question: In this situation, what were you trying to protect: the facts, the relationship, or your own recovery space?
reality_scenes.result_page_depth: Use in Reality Translation cards and Agent follow-up prompts.
reality_scenes.strength: You can make people feel understood, especially when their emotion is obvious but not clearly stated.
reality_scenes.tiny_experiment: Acknowledge the feeling first, then name the kind of support you can realistically offer.
reality_scenes.title: High Empathy, Low Recovery in relationship boundaries
reality_scenes.typical_response: When someone comes to you with emotion and wants time, comfort, or a solution, emotional response and responsibility can blur together, especially with a pattern like High Empathy, Low Recovery.
reality_scenes.why_this_matters: This scene translates your EQ pattern into a concrete everyday moment.

result_page_depth_modules.body: Remember one core pattern, check one evidence point, then choose one action. Do not treat the whole report as a verdict on your personality.
result_page_depth_modules.title: How to read this in 30 seconds

result_page_depth_modules.body: This report looks at the four dimensions, the gaps between them, response quality, and the selected content route. It does not reduce you to one score.
bullets: Dimension signals: SA / ER / EM / RM
Gap signals: strongest dimension and development lever
Route signal: relevant scenes and action prescription
result_page_depth_modules.title: Why this result is interpretable

result_page_depth_modules.body: For the next week, observe three things: whose emotion you notice first, whether you can state a boundary, and how long recovery takes afterward.
result_page_depth_modules.title: Reality check

result_snapshot.continue_path: Save the report, retest later, or ask the Agent about one real scene.
result_snapshot.core_judgment: You read the room quickly, but recovery can lag behind empathy.
result_snapshot.do_not_overread: The first screen is a reading path, not a replacement for the full report.
result_snapshot.evidence_point: Based on the four dimension scores, score gaps, response quality, and mechanism pattern.
result_snapshot.headline: High Empathy, Slow Recovery
result_snapshot.likely_cost: You may carry other people’s pressure with you long after the moment ends.
result_snapshot.minimal_action: Practice understanding without taking over, so empathy is followed by boundary.
result_snapshot.share_safe_sentence: I can understand someone without carrying the whole emotional load.
three_sentence_summary: You read the room quickly, but recovery can lag behind empathy.
You can make people feel understood, especially when emotions run high.
Next move: Practice understanding without taking over, so empathy is followed by boundary.
result_snapshot.top_strength: You can make people feel understood, especially when emotions run high.

scientific_contract.do_not_overread: Do not treat self-report results as external observation or a live behavior record.
scientific_contract.non_ability_statement: EQ-60 explains emotional and relational patterns in self-perception. It is not a certified capability evaluation and should not be used as a high-stakes decision tool. A future scenario module may add judgment data, but it would still not turn the result into a credential.
scientific_contract.non_clinical_statement: This report is not intended for healthcare, treatment, or safety decisions. If distress affects sleep, work, safety, or daily functioning, consult a qualified professional.
scientific_contract.non_hiring_statement: This result should not be used for selection, promotion, rejection, or role assignment. It is better suited for development, communication, and reflection.
scientific_contract.norm_status_statement: Scores currently use provisional norms and versioned interpretation. Percentiles provide a sense of relative standing, not an exact rank. As samples and versions improve, reports retain the version used for comparison over time.
scientific_contract.quality_rules_statement: The report considers response speed, repeated identical choices, extreme choices, neutral choices, and consistency signals to estimate interpretation confidence. Lower confidence does not make the result useless; it means read it more cautiously.
scientific_contract.self_report_statement: This report is based on your responses to 60 items. It explains how you tend to understand, regulate, and respond to emotions in yourself and others. Its value is turning self-perception into reviewable relational signals, action entries, and career-environment variables; it is not direct observation of every live behavior.
scientific_contract.test_definition: This report is based on your responses to 60 items. It explains how you tend to understand, regulate, and respond to emotions in yourself and others. Its value is turning self-perception into reviewable relational signals, action entries, and career-environment variables; it is not direct observation of every live behavior.
scientific_contract.user_safe_summary: Use it for self-understanding, relational reflection, and growth planning.
scientific_contract.version_statement: The report is shaped by item version, scoring version, norm version, and content asset version. Versioning lets retests, support, and editorial review trace how a result was produced.

developing.do_not_overread: The total score locates the reading entry; it does not replace dimensions, confidence, and scene interpretation.
developing.label: Developing
developing.meaning: You have a usable base, but consistency may drop during feedback, pressure, or relational tension.
developing.practice_focus: Pick one frequent scene and prepare one response sentence.
developing.risk: The common trap is knowing something but not using it in the moment.
developing.strength: You already catch part of the signal and can turn it into a routine.

foundational.do_not_overread: The total score locates the reading entry; it does not replace dimensions, confidence, and scene interpretation.
foundational.label: Foundational
foundational.meaning: The current signal suggests starting with clearer recognition, naming, and reset habits.
foundational.practice_focus: Name one emotion daily and pair it with one low-cost reset action.
foundational.risk: Jumping into complex communication tactics may frustrate you if the base signal is unclear.
foundational.strength: Small repeatable actions can create more stable progress.

integrated.do_not_overread: The total score locates the reading entry; it does not replace dimensions, confidence, and scene interpretation.
integrated.label: Integrated
integrated.meaning: The four signals often work together, helping you hold self, others, and the relationship goal in view.
integrated.practice_focus: Build recovery boundaries so stabilizing others does not become your default duty.
integrated.risk: High integration may still create over-responsibility and high-standard fatigue.
integrated.strength: You can systematize strengths into boundaries, repair, and recovery rhythm.

proficient.do_not_overread: The total score locates the reading entry; it does not replace dimensions, confidence, and scene interpretation.
proficient.label: Proficient
proficient.meaning: You can usually recognize, regulate, and handle relational signals with good consistency.
proficient.practice_focus: Move from “I can handle this” to “I choose how to handle this.”
proficient.risk: Others may assume you should always be the stabilizing person.
proficient.strength: You can turn personal strengths into repeatable communication habits.

stable.do_not_overread: The total score locates the reading entry; it does not replace dimensions, confidence, and scene interpretation.
stable.label: Stable
stable.meaning: In many everyday settings, these emotional and relational skills are usable, with specific triggers still visible.
stable.practice_focus: Identify one scene where stability drops and review it afterward.
stable.risk: Stability does not mean no cost, especially in high-intensity relational environments.
stable.strength: Your result can support more precise scene-based practice.

EM.definition: The tendency to understand others’ feelings, perspectives, and unspoken emotional cues.
EM.development_question: What is the other person really concerned about?
EM.do_not_overread: High or low scores are not fixed labels; they describe current self-reported tendencies.
EM.label: Empathy
EM.what_high_can_look_like: You can notice others’ emotional states, while still needing boundaries against over-absorbing.
EM.what_low_can_look_like: You may focus on tasks or facts and miss some emotional cues.

ER.definition: The tendency to steady and recover yourself under pressure, feedback, frustration, or conflict.
ER.development_question: Can I lower the heat first before choosing my response?
ER.do_not_overread: High or low scores are not fixed labels; they describe current self-reported tendencies.
ER.label: Emotion Regulation
ER.what_high_can_look_like: You can often pause, recover, and choose a response under pressure, without being emotionless.
ER.what_low_can_look_like: Recovery may take longer after being triggered, with avoidance, over-explaining, or suppression becoming common.

RM.definition: The tendency to maintain relationship quality through expression, collaboration, boundaries, and repair.
RM.development_question: How can I say this honestly while keeping the conversation open?
RM.do_not_overread: High or low scores are not fixed labels; they describe current self-reported tendencies.
RM.label: Relationship Management
RM.what_high_can_look_like: You can maintain communication and relationship quality, while still watching for honest expression and boundaries.
RM.what_low_can_look_like: Even when feelings are clear, repairing or moving the conversation forward may be harder.

SA.definition: The tendency to notice, name, and make sense of your own emotions.
SA.development_question: What am I actually feeling and needing right now?
SA.do_not_overread: High or low scores are not fixed labels; they describe current self-reported tendencies.
SA.label: Self-Awareness
SA.what_high_can_look_like: You can often identify feelings and triggers quickly, while still needing to turn awareness into action.
SA.what_low_can_look_like: Feelings may become clear only afterward, making it easier to react before understanding.

global_index.definition: The index combines the four dimensions into one overall view of your current emotional and relational pattern.
global_index.how_to_use: Start with the overview, then read dimensions, confidence, and action prescription.
global_index.label: Emotional & Relational Functioning Index
global_index.not_a_capability_score: It is not a credential of live performance and should not be used alone for important decisions.

score_notes.commercial_boundary: The total score is a reading entry; interpretation depends on dimensions, confidence, and scenes.
score_notes.percentile: Percentile explains relative position; it is not a fixed ability label.
score_notes.standard_score: Standard score supports stable display and dimension comparison.

sjt_bridge.description: You are seeing a self-report result. A future scenario module will add how you tend to choose responses in feedback, conflict, boundary, and pressure situations.
sjt_bridge.do_not_overread: There is no public item bank, entry point, or integrated report yet.
sjt_bridge.title: Future scenario module
sjt_bridge.what_it_adds: It supplements self-report with future scenario choices.
sjt_bridge.what_it_is_not: It is not live, not MSCEIT, and not a certified assessment.

guardrails.eq_authority: EQ_60 remains authoritative for emotional and relational self-report signals.
guardrails.source_authority: MBTI, Big Five, and Enneagram remain authoritative only for their own assessment domains.

commercial_depth.fallback_strategy: if selected assets are missing, render formulation + dimension summary + scientific boundary only
commercial_depth.first_screen_strategy: route_headline + evidence label + next best action
commercial_depth.reader_trust_strategy: explain why this route was selected without exposing scoring internals

do_not_overread.en: This is a content route, not a scoring rule or fixed identity label.
do_not_overread.zh-CN: 这是一条内容路由，不是评分规则，也不是固定人格标签。

en.evidence_snapshot_label: Route evidence: core formulation, dimension pattern, response-quality signals, and selected assets.
en.next_best_action: Read the selected scenes first, then practice one prescription for seven days.
en.save_reason: Save this route so you can compare it with real interactions later.
en.why_this_feels_specific: This route organizes your strongest signals as High Empathy, Low Recovery and prioritizes the most relevant mechanisms, scenes, and practices.

zh-CN.evidence_snapshot_label: 本路线依据：核心 formulation、维度组合、质量信号和已选内容资产。
zh-CN.next_best_action: 先读路线中的场景卡，再选择一个行动处方练 7 天。
zh-CN.save_reason: 保存这条路线，之后可对照真实互动验证它是否准确。
zh-CN.why_this_feels_specific: 这个路线把你的主要信号组织为“高共情，低恢复”，并优先展示最相关的机制、场景和行动。

why_this_route_exists.en: This route organizes your strongest signals as High Empathy, Low Recovery and prioritizes the most relevant mechanisms, scenes, and practices.
why_this_route_exists.zh-CN: 这个路线把你的主要信号组织为“高共情，低恢复”，并优先展示最相关的机制、场景和行动。

free_blocks.content:
This assessment is a self‑report questionnaire for self‑reflection and skill development. It is not a medical diagnosis or psychotherapy advice.
Results depend on your current state, interpretation, and response quality.
When distress persists or daily functioning is significantly impaired, professional support is recommended.
free_blocks.title: Important Notice

free_blocks.content: Response quality is **A**: consistency and pacing are stable; interpretability is high.
free_blocks.title: Response Quality

free_blocks.content:
Your overall EQ standard score is **99.5** (population mean 100, SD 15), at the **48.67** percentile.

This assessment covers four capabilities: Self‑Awareness (SA), Emotion Regulation (ER), Social Awareness & Empathy (EM), and Relationship Management (RM). Each section includes a standard score and a maturity band to locate strengths and training priorities.

Response quality: **A**.
free_blocks.title: Overview

free_blocks.content:
**Dimension: Self‑Awareness**

Standard score: **97** (42.07 percentile), band: **Competent**.

You usually identify emotions and connect them to triggers and needs. In many situations you can say things like “I’m tense,” “I need a moment,” or “I felt overlooked.” This reduces misunderstanding and rumination, keeping conversations closer to the real issue.

**Practice**: Before a key conversation, write one sentence: “What matters most to me here?”
free_blocks.title: Self‑Awareness · Competent

free_blocks.content:
**Dimension: Emotion Regulation**

Standard score: **83** (12.85 percentile), band: **Baseline**.

Under pressure or triggers, emotions can take over the action system—impulses, rumination, sleep disruption, or prolonged tension. This is not a “willpower” label; it reflects a toolbox that isn’t automatic yet. Regulation aims to bring intensity and duration back into a manageable range, not to erase emotions.

**90‑second reset**: Pause → slow exhale 6 times → feel your feet on the ground to create space before reacting.
free_blocks.title: Emotion Regulation · Baseline

free_blocks.content:
**Dimension: Social Awareness & Empathy**

Standard score: **119** (89.74 percentile), band: **Exceptional**.

Your empathy is exceptionally strong. Even in noisy environments you read emotions and motives accurately and respond with warmth. This is a foundation of influence. Keep boundaries to prevent emotional overload.

**Practice**: Use: “I see this is hard for you. I can stay with you for a bit,” while keeping your limits.
free_blocks.title: Social Awareness & Empathy · Exceptional

free_blocks.content:
**Dimension: Relationship Management**

Standard score: **99** (47.34 percentile), band: **Competent**.

You communicate and collaborate well, maintain trust, and manage disagreements without breaking the relationship. You tend to balance authenticity with relationship care.

**Practice**: In conflict, align on the shared goal first, then discuss options.
free_blocks.title: Relationship Management · Competent

free_blocks.content:
**Primary profile: Compassion Overload (High Empathy + Low Regulation)**

Your results show Empathy **119** very high and Emotion Regulation **83** lower. You feel others’ pain and stress strongly, and group climate can affect you quickly. The gift is warmth and attunement; the cost is overload and blurry boundaries.

**Typical strengths**
- High resonance: people open up to you easily.
- Strong ‘room reading’: you sense shifts and hidden tension.
- Care motive: a real drive to help.

**Typical costs**
- Emotional contagion: others’ feelings enter your body and drain sleep/energy.
- Rescue reflex: urgency to fix, leading to over‑involvement.
- Boundary loss: ‘empathy’ turns into ‘carrying,’ then burnout.

**Key shift: bounded care**
High empathy doesn’t require self‑sacrifice. Training focuses on three boundaries:
1) **Emotional boundary**: “This is theirs” vs “this is mine.”
2) **Time boundary**: define how long you can be available.
3) **Action boundary**: move from ‘fixing’ to ‘helping them see choices.’

**Support scripts**
- “I hear this is hard. I can stay with you for 10 minutes to sort it out.”
- “What I can offer is … The deeper choice is yours.”
- “I care about you, and I also need to protect my energy.”

**What this free report gives you**
- Boundary training to turn empathy into a resource.
- Regulation tools to prevent overload spillover.
- 14‑day plan for sustainable helping.
free_blocks.title: Compassion Overload

free_blocks.content:
**14‑Day Plan: Boundaried Care (10–15 min/day)**

Goal: keep your warmth and sensitivity while building emotional, time, and action boundaries to prevent overload.

| Day | Theme | Practice |
|---|---|---|
| Day 1 | Mine vs yours | Note one contagion moment: whose emotion is it? what can I do? |
| Day 2 | Emotional boundary | Practice: “I see it’s hard for you. This is your feeling. I’m here with you.” |
| Day 3 | Time boundary | Set a time cap (10/20 min) and write an ending script. |
| Day 4 | Action boundary | Shift from fixing to options: write 3 guiding questions. |
| Day 5 | Back to body | 2‑minute tactile grounding (cup/table texture). |
| Day 6 | Self‑compassion | Write 3 lines: this is hard; I’m not alone; I’ll be kind to myself. |
| Day 7 | Support & referral | List 3 resources you can hand off to; don’t carry alone. |
| Day 8 | Empathize without spiraling | Reflect feeling, then ask: listen or brainstorm? |
| Day 9 | Energy return | After deep listening: 5 minutes walk/breath to reset. |
| Day10 | State limits | Use: “I can stay up to here; then I need rest.” once. |
| Day11 | Load monitoring | Rate emotional load 0–10; above 7, reduce inputs intentionally. |
| Day12 | One smallest act | Keep helping action to 1 minimal step; avoid over‑involvement. |
| Day13 | Relationship structure | Pick one draining relationship: what boundary makes it sustainable? |
| Day14 | Consolidate | 3 boundary principles + 3 care scripts. |

Boundaries aren’t coldness—they make care sustainable.
free_blocks.title: Boundaried Care

free_blocks.content:
**Scoring (Production v1.0)**

1) 60 items use a 5‑point scale; reverse‑keyed items are flipped before aggregation (6 − response).
2) Each quadrant has 15 items. Raw sums are mapped to standard scores (mean 100, SD 15).
3) Response‑quality indicators are computed (time, long‑stringing, extreme/neutral bias, internal consistency). Only A/B quality enters the norm pool.
4) This free report includes cross‑quadrant insights and a 14‑day training plan to convert understanding into practice.
free_blocks.title: Methodology

free_blocks.content:
**How to use this report**: Treat this as guidance for self‑reflection and skill training. A retake interval of at least 14 days improves stability.
This platform does not use a single test result for labeling decisions.
free_blocks.title: Closing Notes
